question:
B-1. Evaluate
\[
\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n} \sum_{k=1}^{n}\left(\left[\frac{2 n}{k}\right]-2\left[\frac{n}{k}\right]\right)
\]
and express your answer in the form \( \log a-b \), with \( a \) and \( b \) positive integers.
Here \( [x] \) is defined to be the integer such that \( [x] \leqq x<[x]+1 \) and \( \log x \) is the logarithm of \( x \) to base \( e \).
solution:
B-1.
It is shown below that \( a=4 \) and \( b=1 \). Let \( f(x)=[2 / x]-2[1 / x] \). Then the desired limit \( L \) equals \( \int_{0}^{1} f(x) d x \). For \( n=1,2, \ldots, f(x)=0 \) on \( 2 /(2 n+1)<x \leqq 1 / n \) and \( f(x)=1 \) on \( 1 /(n+1)<x \leqq 2 /(2 n+1) \). Hence
\[
\begin{aligned}
L & =\left(\frac{2}{3}-\frac{2}{4}\right)+\left(\frac{2}{5}-\frac{2}{6}\right)+\cdots=-1+2\left(1-\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}-\cdots\right) \\
& =-1+2 \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d x}{1+x}=-1+2 \ln 2=\ln 4-1
\end{aligned}
\]

descriptive_long.question:
B-1. Evaluate
\[
\lim _{totalsteps \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{totalsteps} \sum_{indexiter=1}^{totalsteps}\left(\left[\frac{2 totalsteps}{indexiter}\right]-2\left[\frac{totalsteps}{indexiter}\right]\right)
\]
and express your answer in the form \( \log constalpha-constbeta \), with \( constalpha \) and \( constbeta \) positive integers.
Here \( [realvalue] \) is defined to be the integer such that \( [realvalue] \leqq realvalue<[realvalue]+1 \) and \( \log realvalue \) is the logarithm of \( realvalue \) to base \( e \).
descriptive_long.solution:
B-1.
It is shown below that \( constalpha=4 \) and \( constbeta=1 \). Let \( piecefunc(realvalue)=[2 / realvalue]-2[1 / realvalue] \). Then the desired limit \( limitvalue \) equals \( \int_{0}^{1} piecefunc(realvalue) d realvalue \). For \( totalsteps=1,2, \ldots, piecefunc(realvalue)=0 \) on \( 2 /(2 totalsteps+1)<realvalue \leqq 1 / totalsteps \) and \( piecefunc(realvalue)=1 \) on \( 1 /(totalsteps+1)<realvalue \leqq 2 /(2 totalsteps+1) \). Hence
\[
\begin{aligned}
limitvalue & =\left(\frac{2}{3}-\frac{2}{4}\right)+\left(\frac{2}{5}-\frac{2}{6}\right)+\cdots=-1+2\left(1-\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}-\cdots\right) \\
& =-1+2 \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d realvalue}{1+realvalue}=-1+2 \ln 2=\ln 4-1
\end{aligned}
\]

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
B-1. Evaluate
\[
\lim _{riverboat \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{riverboat} \sum_{sandcastle=1}^{riverboat}\left(\left[\frac{2 riverboat}{sandcastle}\right]-2\left[\frac{riverboat}{sandcastle}\right]\right)
\]
and express your answer in the form \( \log sailplane-scarecrow \), with \( sailplane \) and \( scarecrow \) positive integers.
Here \( [drumstick] \) is defined to be the integer such that \( [drumstick] \leqq drumstick<[drumstick]+1 \) and \( \log drumstick \) is the logarithm of \( drumstick \) to base \( e \).
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
B-1.
It is shown below that \( sailplane=4 \) and \( scarecrow=1 \). Let \( moonlight(drumstick)=[2 / drumstick]-2[1 / drumstick] \). Then the desired limit \( daffodils \) equals \( \int_{0}^{1} moonlight(drumstick) d drumstick \). For \( riverboat=1,2, \ldots, moonlight(drumstick)=0 \) on \( 2 /(2 riverboat+1)<drumstick \leqq 1 / riverboat \) and \( moonlight(drumstick)=1 \) on \( 1 /(riverboat+1)<drumstick \leqq 2 /(2 riverboat+1) \). Hence
\[
\begin{aligned}
daffodils & =\left(\frac{2}{3}-\frac{2}{4}\right)+\left(\frac{2}{5}-\frac{2}{6}\right)+\cdots=-1+2\left(1-\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}-\cdots\right) \\
& =-1+2 \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d drumstick}{1+drumstick}=-1+2 \ln 2=\ln 4-1
\end{aligned}
\]

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
B-1. Evaluate
\[
\lim _{\microsize \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{\microsize} \sum_{\vastindex=1}^{\microsize}\left(\left[\frac{2 \microsize}{\vastindex}\right]-2\left[\frac{\microsize}{\vastindex}\right]\right)
\]
and express your answer in the form \( \log \nothingness-\infinity \), with \( \nothingness \) and \( \infinity \) positive integers.
Here \( [constantval] \) is defined to be the integer such that \( [constantval] \leqq constantval<[constantval]+1 \) and \( \log constantval \) is the logarithm of constantval to base \( e \).
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
B-1.
It is shown below that \( \nothingness=4 \) and \( \infinity=1 \). Let \( steadyvalue(constantval)=[2 / constantval]-2[1 / constantval] \). Then the desired limit \( unbounded \) equals \( \int_{0}^{1} steadyvalue(constantval) d constantval \). For \( microsize=1,2, \ldots, steadyvalue(constantval)=0 \) on \( 2 /(2 microsize+1)<constantval \leqq 1 / microsize \) and \( steadyvalue(constantval)=1 \) on \( 1 /(microsize+1)<constantval \leqq 2 /(2 microsize+1) \). Hence
\[
\begin{aligned}
unbounded & =\left(\frac{2}{3}-\frac{2}{4}\right)+\left(\frac{2}{5}-\frac{2}{6}\right)+\cdots=-1+2\left(1-\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}-\cdots\right) \\
& =-1+2 \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d constantval}{1+constantval}=-1+2 \ln 2=\ln 4-1
\end{aligned}
\]

garbled_string.question:
B-1. Evaluate
\[
\lim _{vjzktmra \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{vjzktmra} \sum_{qlxnsdwe=1}^{vjzktmra}\left(\left[\frac{2 vjzktmra}{qlxnsdwe}\right]-2\left[\frac{vjzktmra}{qlxnsdwe}\right]\right)
\]
and express your answer in the form \( \log smctbyez-gqphdavr \), with \( smctbyez \) and \( gqphdavr \) positive integers.
Here \( [hrmgpltu] \) is defined to be the integer such that \( [hrmgpltu] \leqq hrmgpltu<[hrmgpltu]+1 \) and \( \log hrmgpltu \) is the logarithm of \( hrmgpltu \) to base \( e \).
garbled_string.solution:
B-1.
It is shown below that \( smctbyez=4 \) and \( gqphdavr=1 \). Let \( zbpkxqiu(hrmgpltu)=[2 / hrmgpltu]-2[1 / hrmgpltu] \). Then the desired limit \( uwhcdgya \) equals \( \int_{0}^{1} zbpkxqiu(hrmgpltu) \, d hrmgpltu \). For \( vjzktmra=1,2, \ldots, zbpkxqiu(hrmgpltu)=0 \) on \( 2 /(2 vjzktmra+1)<hrmgpltu \leqq 1 / vjzktmra \) and \( zbpkxqiu(hrmgpltu)=1 \) on \( 1 /(vjzktmra+1)<hrmgpltu \leqq 2 /(2 vjzktmra+1) \). Hence
\[
\begin{aligned}
uwhcdgya & =\left(\frac{2}{3}-\frac{2}{4}\right)+\left(\frac{2}{5}-\frac{2}{6}\right)+\cdots=-1+2\left(1-\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}-\cdots\right) \\
& =-1+2 \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d hrmgpltu}{1+hrmgpltu}=-1+2 \ln 2=\ln 4-1
\end{aligned}
\]

kernel_variant.question:
Let

a_n = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{k=1}^{n}\Bigl(\bigl\lfloor \tfrac{2n}{k}\bigr\rfloor-2\bigl\lfloor \tfrac{n}{k}\bigr\rfloor\Bigr), \qquad n\ge 1.

Evaluate the limit

\[\lim_{n\to\infty}a_n\]

and express your answer in the form

\[\ln a\; -\; b, \qquad a,b\in\mathbb Z_{>0},\]

where \(\lfloor \cdot \rfloor\) denotes the floor function and \(\ln\) is the natural logarithm.
kernel_variant.solution:
Throughout put
\[
S_n:=\frac1n\sum_{k=1}^{n}\Bigl(\lfloor 2n/k\rfloor-2\lfloor n/k\rfloor\Bigr),\qquad n\ge 1.
\]
We shall prove that
\[
\lim_{n\to\infty}S_n =\ln 4-1.
\]

1.  Passing to an integral.

Write \(x=k/n\;(0<x\le 1)\).  Then \(k=nx\) and
\[
S_n = \sum_{k=1}^{n}\bigl(\lfloor 2/x\rfloor-2\lfloor 1/x\rfloor\bigr)\,\frac1n
     \xrightarrow[n\to\infty]{} L:=\int_{0}^{1}f(x)\,dx,\qquad
f(x):=\lfloor 2/x\rfloor-2\lfloor 1/x\rfloor.
\]
Because \(f\) is bounded and piece-wise constant, Riemann convergence is immediate.

2.  The structure of \(f\).

Fix an integer \(m\ge 1\) and consider \(x\in(1/(m+1),1/m]\).  Then
\(\lfloor 1/x\rfloor=m\), while \(2/x\in[2m,2m+2)\); consequently
\[
\lfloor 2/x\rfloor =\begin{cases}2m+1,&x\le 2/(2m+1),\\[4pt]2m,&x>2/(2m+1).\end{cases}
\]
Hence
\[
f(x)=\begin{cases}1,&x\in\bigl(\tfrac1{m+1},\,\tfrac{2}{2m+1}\bigr],\\[6pt]0,&x\in\bigl(\tfrac{2}{2m+1},\,\tfrac1m\bigr].\end{cases}
\]

3.  Computing the integral.

For every \(m\ge 1\)
\[
\int_{1/(m+1)}^{1/m}f(x)\,dx 
      =\frac{2}{2m+1}-\frac{1}{m+1} 
      =\frac{1}{(2m+1)(m+1)}.  
\]
Therefore
\[
L=\sum_{m=1}^{\infty}\Bigl(\frac{2}{2m+1}-\frac{1}{m+1}\Bigr)
  =\sum_{m=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{(2m+1)(m+1)}.  \tag{1}
\]

4.  Transforming the series.

Retain the partial sums to avoid subtracting divergent series term-by-term.  Let
\(
H_N:=\sum_{k=1}^{N}\!\frac1k\) be the \(N\)-th harmonic number.   For \(N\ge 1\) set
\[
S_N:=\sum_{m=1}^{N}\Bigl(\frac{2}{2m+1}-\frac{1}{m+1}\Bigr).
\]
Write the odd-reciprocal block explicitly:
\[
\sum_{m=1}^{N}\frac{2}{2m+1}=2\Bigl(\sum_{n=0}^{N}\frac{1}{2n+1}-1\Bigr)
                 =2\sum_{n=0}^{N}\frac{1}{2n+1}-2.
\]
Since \(\sum_{m=1}^{N}\frac{1}{m+1}=H_{N+1}-1\), we have
\[
S_N=2\sum_{n=0}^{N}\frac{1}{2n+1}-2-H_{N+1}+1
   =2\sum_{n=0}^{N}\frac{1}{2n+1}-H_{N+1}-1.  \tag{2}
\]
Now decompose \(H_{2N+1}\):
\[
H_{2N+1}=\sum_{n=0}^{N}\frac{1}{2n+1}+\sum_{n=1}^{N}\frac{1}{2n}
        =\sum_{n=0}^{N}\frac{1}{2n+1}+\frac12 H_{N}.  \tag{3}
\]
Solve (3) for the odd block and substitute in (2):
\[
S_N =2\bigl(H_{2N+1}-\tfrac12 H_N\bigr)-H_{N+1}-1
    =2H_{2N+1}-H_N-H_{N+1}-1. \tag{4}
\]

5.  Taking the limit.

Using the well-known asymptotic expansion
\(H_M = \ln M + \gamma + O(M^{-1})\) (\(\gamma\) Euler's constant), insert \(M=2N+1,\,N,\,N+1\) into (4):
\[
S_N = 2\bigl(\ln(2N+1)+\gamma\bigr)-\bigl(\ln N+\gamma\bigr)-\bigl(\ln(N+1)+\gamma\bigr)-1+o(1).
\]
Since \(\ln(2N+1)=\ln 2+\ln N+o(1)\) and \(\ln(N+1)=\ln N+o(1)\), cancellation of \(\ln N\) and of \(\gamma\) leaves
\[
S_N = 2\ln 2 -1 + o(1).
\]
Hence
\[
L=\lim_{N\to\infty}S_N =2\ln 2 -1 = \ln 4 -1.
\]

6.  Conclusion.

\[\boxed{\displaystyle\lim_{n\to\infty}a_n = \ln 4-1}\]
Thus the requested integers are \(a=4\) and \(b=1\).

core_steps: Convert the averaged sum into a Riemann sum by setting x = k/n, so [2n/k] → [2/x] and [n/k] → [1/x].
Define f(x) = [2/x] − 2[1/x]; the limit becomes ∫₀¹ f(x) dx.
Locate break-points where the floors jump; on each interval (1/(n+1),1/n] the integrand is constant (0 or 1).
Add the interval lengths where f(x)=1, obtaining a telescoping alternating series that equals −1 + 2∑ₙ (−1)^{n−1}/n.
Recognize the series as 2∫₀¹ dx/(1+x)=2 ln 2 and conclude ln 4 − 1.

slot1.description: Common positive integer coefficient replacing the two occurrences of ‘2’:  [αn/k] − α[n/k]

slot2.description: Chosen base of the logarithm that appears in the final answer (and in the integral of 1/(1+x)); changing it merely rescales the log term.
